arouse: something about the man aroused the guard's suspicions  (evoke)
an ability to influence the audience and to arouse the masses. (emotion)
I was surprised to find that this look aroused me.(excite)
awoken: i was awoken by a loud bang
standing next to: being located immediately beside or very close to someone or something
He was standing next to his car.
he's always standing next to me , no matter what
God standing next to me, no matter the situation.
assess: evaluate or estimate the nature, ability, or quality of
the committee must assess the relative importance of the issues
calculate or estimate the price or value of:
the damage was assessed at £5 billion
awkward: causing difficulty; hard to do or deal with
mission statement: a formal summary of the aims and values of a company, organization, or individual
the company's mission statement emphasizes its commitment to quality and customer satisfaction
stand: to be in an upright position on your feet
He was standing near the door.
stand out (verb phrase): It means to be easily noticed or different from others.
be clearly better or more significant than someone or something
She stands out because of her confidence.
This CV will stand out among the others.
Bright colors help the building stand out.
standout (adjective or noun): his describes something exceptional, noticeable, or outstanding
he became a standout quarterback in the NFL
crowd (la foule): a large number of people gathered together in a disorganized or unruly way
gathering: Understood : (I gathered that he was unhappy.)
Carried out: to do, perform, or complete a task, plan, or duty.
To carried out an awkward task
The experiment was carried out successfully.
to come up: Something appears or occurs, often unexpectedly. (An issue came up during the meeting.)
To be mentioned or discussed (His name came up in the discussion)
To approach or move closer (She came up to me and said hello.)
To appear (exam, opportunity, job) (A job opportunity came up.)
To rise or move upward (The sun came up)
further: Further = additional / more (No further action is required.)
Further = at a greater distance (The village is further than I thought.)
Further (verb) = to help something develop (The training will further your career) .
withdraw: Take money out (bank / ATM) (I withdrew cash from the ATM.)
To remove or take away (something) (He withdrew his hand quickly.) (She withdrew from the conversation.)
Leave or stop participating (The team withdrew from the competition.)
Remove something officially (The company withdrew the faulty product from the market.)
point out: to draw attention to something, to indicate, or to mention something clearly.
He pointed out the mistake in the report.
She pointed out a flaw in his argument.
an odd: That's an odd way to solve the problem.
blurded (verb): make or become unclear or less distinct (it's an action that causes something to become less clear or less focused)
the lines of the painting are blurred
blury (adjective): something is by nature unclear or not in focus (it's a state or condition of being unclear or not sharp)
stationery: she bought some new stationery for writing letters
fares: the money a passenger on public transportation has to pay
perks: benefits received in addition to regular pay
Letting: Letting' is another way of saying renting
venue: the place where an event happens
Venue is another way to talk about places for meetings
eg: The concert venue can hold 5,000 people
buried: sometimes when you are in the dark place , you think you have been buried but actually you've been plainted
clues: a piece of evidence or information used in the detection of a crime or solving of a mystery
the police are still looking for clues in the case
hanging up the phone: to end a phone call by putting the receiver back in its place or pressing the end call button on a mobile phone.
After finishing the conversation, she hung up the phone.
stockbroker: a person who buys and sells stocks and other securities on behalf of clients
He works as a stockbroker at a financial firm.
sheet: a large piece of cloth used as a covering for a bed
while the word 'paper' is uncountable, it is possible to count the word 'sheet'
fulfill: (remplir) , acheave or complete (a task, duty, or role) satisfactorily
She fulfilled her dream of becoming a doctor.
The company fulfilled its promise to deliver the product on time.
